rulings.Case Title: LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC VS. MARTIN LEE ENG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Vacate Void Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 25, 2021, Line 2 DEFENDANT MARTIN ENG'S Motion To Vacate Void Judgment Off calendar. Defendant did not serve the motion at least 16 court days before the hearing, plus 5 days for service by mail, as required by Code of Civil Procedure section 1005. In any event, plaintiff presents no coherent grounds for the Court to void the judgment entered against him on July 8, 2015, which has long since become final and has been the subject of extensive post-judgment enforcement proceedings. The motion is frivolous. It should not be refiled. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: KATHERINE WALKER VS. PHOENIX RISING ENTERPRISES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment; Declaration Of Frank A. Cialone
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 25, 2021, Line 3, CROSS DEFENDANT JERRY OSTRIN'S Motion For Entry Of Judgment Cross-Defendant Jerry Ostrin's motion for entry of judgment is granted. By order filed December 15, 2016, the court sustained Mr. Ostrin's demurrer to the cross-complaint without leave to amend, but with permission to refile those claims in the family law case between Ms. Zolfaghari and Mr. Ostrin [In re Marriage of Jerry Ostrin and Kathia Zolfaghari, Case No. FDI-15-784382], and stayed all causes of action in the cross-complaint alleged against Mr. Ostrin pending the conclusion of the family law case. The order provided that if any of the causes of action of the cross-complaint have not been fully adjudicated in the family law case, cross-complainants may request that the stay be lifted. Thereafter, on March 29, 2021, the family law court entered an order granting Mr. Ostrin's motion for determination of good faith settlement and judgment on the pleadings in the instant action. That court found that Mr. Ostrin's settlement with Cross-Complainant Katherine Walker was made in good faith within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure section 877.6, and that its order "shall bar any person, including Zolfaghari and PRE, from any claims against Ostrin for contribution, indemnity, or other liability that arises out of or relates in any way to the claims asserted by Walker. Accordingly, Ostrin is entitled to judgment on the pleadings in the Walker Litigation, and the Cross-Complaint against him shall be dismissed with prejudice." Defendants and Cross-Complainants' late-filed and mistitled opposition misstates the legal effect of a good faith determination under section 877.6 and of the family court's order, which is dispositive. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for Mr. Ostrin is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and a proposed judgment and must email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: MARILYN A. BARTHOLOMEW VS. GOLDEN GATE TOW, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Revoking And Or Setting Aside A Stipulation Order And A Discovery Order; ***Court Reporter Fees Paid***
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 25, 2021, Line 1, 1- Plaintiff's Motion For An Order Revoking And Or Setting Aside A Stipulation Order And A Discovery Order. Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion GRANTED in part as follows. The tentative ruling that I transmitted to the Clerk for the hearing on April 13, 2021, included "hearing required," but that language was not included in the online tentative ruling system. This omission was not brought to my attention prior to issuing the Order filed on April 16, 2021. Judicial officers must provide parties the full right to be heard according to law, and there is a difference between a party failing to appear after being ordered to appear (what I thought happened) and a party choosing not to appear when there was no such order (what actually happened). Therefore, the request to vacate the Order filed on April 16, 2021 is GRANTED. CCP 473, 1008. Prior to the hearing on the motion for reconsideration, the parties should meet and confer to determine whether they will argue the underlying motion at the hearing on May 25. If they agree to do so, I will hear argument on that motion. If they do not agree to do so, the parties should be prepared to discuss scheduling a new hearing on the underlying motion. I do not have authority to grant or deny a request to withdraw the stipulation to me acting as judge pro tem; such a motion "must" be heard by the Presiding Judge or his designee. CRC 2.816(e). As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD CLARK VS. IMPERFECT FOODS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 25, 2021, Line 4, DEFENDANTS IMPERFECT FOODS, INC., BENJAMIN SIMON, BENJAMIN CHESLER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(t), the parties stipulated to and the Court approved the instant hearing on the parties' cross-motions for summary adjudication to resolve several issues regarding the character of plaintiff's shares in defendant Imperfect. The Court rules as follows: 1. Whether Plaintiff's "Unvested" Shares of restricted stock of Imperfect Foods constitute wages under California law. The Court grants summary adjudication in favor of defendants on issue 1 2. Whether the Forfeiture provision set forth in Section 2(a) of the Restricted Stock Agreement entered into between Plaintiff and Imperfect Foods providing for the automatic reversion and forfeiture of Plaintiff's "Unvested" Shares of restricted stock back to Imperfect Foods upon Plaintiff's termination without payment to Plaintiff, is valid and enforceable, or whether such forfeiture and reversion of the "Unvested" Shares back to Imperfect Foods without payment constitutes an illegal wage forfeiture. Based on the above, the Court grants summary adjudication in favor of defendants on issue 2. Issues 3 and 5 are related and the Court analyzes them together. 3. Whether Plaintiff's Vested Shares of restricted stock of Imperfect Foods constitute wages under California law. 5. Whether the Purchase Option provision set forth in Section 2(b) of the Restricted Stock Agreement entered into between Plaintiff and Imperfect Foods, providing Imperfect Foods the right and option to purchase Plaintiff's Vested Shares at a price of $0.0001/share in the event Plaintiff ceased to be "Engaged for Cause" is valid and enforceable, or whether such purchase of Vested Shares at a price of $0.0001/share constitutes an illegal wage forfeiture. Here, the Court denies summary adjudication on both parties' cross-motions as to issue 3. The The Court grants summary adjudication in favor of defendant on issue 5. 4. Whether the conversion of 229,137 shares of Plaintiff's Vested Shares to "Unvested" shares pursuant to the Amendment to the Restricted Stock Agreement entered into between Plaintiff and Imperfect Foods is valid and enforceable, or whether the conversion of those Vested Shares to "Unvested" Shares constitutes an illegal wage forfeiture. The Court grants summary adjudication in favor of defendant on issue 4. *THE COURTS FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO ALL COUNSEL* As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND VINCENT DIGIACOMO VS. RECOLOGY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Recology, Inc. To Further Respond To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories, Set One, Special Interrogatories, Set One, Demand For Production, Set One, And, Demand For Production, Set Four And Imposing Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 25, 2021, Line 3, PLAINTIFF RAYMOND DIGIACOMO's Motion For Order Compelling Recology, Inc. To Further Respond To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories, Set One, Special Interrogatories, Set One, Demand For Production, Set One, And, Demand For Production, Set Four And Imposing Monetary Sanction (Part 1 of 2 for purposes of entry of Tentative Ruling). Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. FROG 15.1 - DENIED. The amended response is Code compliant. SPROG 1, 2, 3, 4 - GRANTED. The "financial loss or gain" that Recology would incur if it changed collection times or processes is relevant to assessing burdens in relation to the requested injunction. Recology's objection that it cannot calculate this information is not credible. If Recology cannot answer without expert testimony, it should amend its answer to so state. The objection based on Civil Code 3295 appears inapplicable because neither the second nor third amended seeks exemplary damages. SRPG 5 - DENIED. Recology answered this information in response to the series 4 form interrogatories. SPROG 6 - GRANTED. This is a routine interrogatory. RFP Set 1. RFP 3 - DENIED. Recology stated that it would produce. RFP 4 - DENIED. The limitation is reasonable. RFP 5 - DENIED. The statement that Recology has no documents is Code compliant. RFP Set 4. RFP 2 - GRANTED. Recology's objections are nonspecific and insufficient to oppose the request. RFP 3 - DENIED. The request for "any and all documents which describe " is overbroad. RFP 4 - DENIED. This is irrelevant to the merits of the action and Plaintiff has not shown why discovery about discovery is necessary or appropriate. RFP 6 - GRANTED in part. Recology must produce documents sufficient to show (as opposed to any and all) its corporate policy for responding to trash collection noise complaints. RFP 7-13 GRANTED. A plaintiff is entitled to materials on which a defendant based its affirmative defenses. The RFPs are corollaries to RFP 15.1 which requires identification of documents that support affirmative defenses. Recology need not produce publicly available materials such as municipal codes but must revise its responses to so indicate. The requests for sanctions by both sides are DENIED. The motion is granted in part and denied in part, so I find both parties acted with substantial justification. (Tentative Ruling continues in Part 2 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ARF FINANCIAL, LLC VS. LOD BALLPARK BUFFET, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And For Leave To Defend The Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 25, 2021, Line 6, DEFENDANT NICK BOVIS' Motion To Set Aside Default And For Leave To Defend The Action Defendant Bovis's motion to set aside the default entered against him on May 12, 2020 is granted. Although defendant shows that Bovis was personally served with the summons and complaint on April 2, 2020, under the general orders of the Court issued during the COVID-19 emergency, the period from March 18, 2020 to June 1, 2020 were deemed court holidays for purposes of computing time for filing papers with the court, including the time to answer or otherwise respond to a complaint. Accordingly, Defendant's responsive pleading was not yet due on May 12, 2020, and it was an error for his default to be entered. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: BETSY PONCE VS. PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Supplemental Interrogatory
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 25, 2021, Line 4, DEFENDANT ALVAH CONTRACTORS, INC.'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Supplemental Interrogatory. Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion GRANTED. Plaintiff cannot logically say that none of her responses have changed and also say that Defendant should review prior deposition transcripts. The reference to additional information in the depositions suggests that the responses have changed. If the depositions did not make a difference, it would be unnecessary to refer to them. Thus, the answer is evasive. Moreover, Plaintiff's Second Amended Response repeats baseless objections such as that the supplemental interrogatory is "premature" (it is not) or that it calls for disclosure of expert information (which is not a valid objection to a supplemental interrogatory). Sanctions GRANTED in the amount of $4,020. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: EMILIE M. CALHOUN VS. DEXTER CHAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Appointment Of Neutral Arbitrator
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 25, 2021, Line 7, DEFENDANTS DEXTER CHAN, APRIL CHAN'S Petition For Appointment Of Neutral Arbitrator By order filed December 17, 2020, the Court granted defendants' motion to compel arbitration under their fee agreement with plaintiff attorney. The agreement provides, "The parties shall appoint one person or agree upon a 3-person panel to hear and determine the dispute, except that if the parties cannot agree, then the Superior Court of San Francisco shall choose an impartial arbitrator whose decision shall be final and conclusive on all parties." Plaintiff shows that she has proposed that Defendants select one of two alternative dispute resolution organizations, JAMS or ADR Services, or propose their own forum, and that Defendants have not responded. Plaintiff also shows that on January 15, 2021, she proposed three arbitrators at JAMS, and that on February 26, 2021, Defendants responded, "We found your list unacceptable and we are not limiting our search to any specific organization." Defendants have not proposed any arbitrators, and their attempts to schedule the arbitration before an arbitrator or a forum have been selected are premature. In the interest of moving the arbitration forward, the Court rules as follows: By June 1, 2021, Defendants may file and serve a list of three proposed arbitrators. The Court thereafter will follow the procedure specified in Code of Civil Procedure section 1281.6: it will issue an order nominating five persons from the parties' lists or, if defendants fail to timely submit a list of proposed arbitrators, from other sources. Within five days of receipt of notice of the nominees from the Court, the parties must jointly select the arbitrator, whether or not the arbitrator is among the nominees. If the parties fail to do so, the Court will appoint the arbitrator from the nominees. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ANA MORLEY VS. STEVEN P. OKUHN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Corrected Version Of The March 31, 2021, Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery Requests And Impose Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 25, 2021, Line 6, PLAINTIFF ANA MORLEY's Corrected Version Of The March 31, 2021, Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery Requests And Impose Sanctions. Matter to be heard by Judge Pro Tem Aaron Minnis at 1:30 p.m. The Pro Tem Judge has emailed a Zoom invitation directly to the parties for the hearing. Judge Pro Tem Aaron Minnis has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, in part. The motion is timely because it was electronically served on March 31, 2021, the last date per the parties' stipulation. See Young Decl Para. 13:15-18. The motion is granted as to requests for production relating to the parties' jointly-titled financial statements; the Macondray residence; the parties jointly-titled accounts, assets, and liabilities; and communications by or between the parties relating thereto. Within 30 days, Defendant shall provide verified, Code-compliant responses and produce documents. Where no documents exist, Defendant shall affirm that a diligent search and a reasonable inquiry has been made. CCP 2031.230. Defendant shall provide a privilege log and counsel for the parties shall promptly meet and confer regarding a protective order. As to the remaining requests, the motion is denied without prejudice. The requests are overbroad and seek vast information about every facet of Defendant's finances individually, and in some instances the finances of third parties, which is premature given the pleadings are to be amended, the absence of a protective order, and the lack of sufficient meet and confer by counsel to narrow the issues in dispute prior to filing the instant motion. Moreover, a right of privacy exists as to a party's or third party's confidential financial affairs, even when the information sought is admittedly relevant to the litigation. Cobb v. Sup.Ct. (Tleel) (1979) 99 CA3d 543, 550. The party seeking discovery must show a particularized need for the confidential information sought and that the information is directly relevant to a cause of action or defense, i.e., that it is essential to determining the truth of the matters in dispute (Britt v. Sup.Ct. (San Diego Unified Port Dist.) (1978) 20 C3d 844, 859-862), which Plaintiff has not shown. The request for sanctions is denied. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. The Pro Tem Judge has emailed a Zoom invitation directly to the parties for the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to aaron@minnisandsmallets.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: OLD REPUBLIC NATIONAL TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. D 27 LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Old Republic Title Company'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of D 27 Llc'S First Amended Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 25, 2021, Line 11, CROSS DEFENDANTS OLD REPUBLIC NATIONAL TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY, OLD REPUBLIC TITLE COMPANY'S Motion To Strike Portions Of D 27 Llc'S First Amended Cross-Complaint Old Republic Title's alternative motion to strike punitive damages from the FACC is denied. Punitive damages may be awarded against an escrow agent for violating escrow instructions or concealing information from a party to the escrow, which are among the allegations D 27 brings against Old Republic Title here. (Virtanen v. O'Connell (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 688, 714-715; Moe v. Transamerica Title Ins. Co. (1971) 21 Cal.App.3d 289, 303.) Old Republic Title's alternative motion to strike attorney fees under the "tort of another doctrine" is denied. "A person who through the tort of another has been required to act in the protection of his interests by bringing or defending an action against a third person is entitled to recover compensation for the reasonably necessary loss of time, attorney's fees, and other expenditures thereby suffered or incurred." (Prentice v. North American Title Guaranty (1963) 59 Cal.2d 618, 620.) More specifically, "[w]hen a paid escrow holder has, as in this case, negligently made it necessary for the vendor of land to file a quiet title action against a third person, attorney's fees incurred by the vendor in prosecuting such action are recoverable as an item of the vendor's damages in an action against the escrow holder." (Id. at 621; see also Tung v. Chicago Title Company (2021) 63 Cal.App.5th 734, at *6.) Here, similarly, D 27 alleges that as a result of Old Republic's negligence, it was required to file suit against the seller to resolve the missing parcel claim, and incurred attorney's fees and costs in so doing. (FACC par. 14.) Old Republic Title concedes that D 27's prayer for attorney's fees in that prior action is proper. (Mot. at 17:22-26.) Further, defendant Old Republic National Title Insurance Company (ORNTIC) is a third party for purposes of this rule. In any event, Old Republic Title's request that the Court strike a portion of that prayer to the extent that it seeks recovery of fees incurred in this action as against it is not a proper subject for a motion to strike. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for D 27 is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 25, 2021, Line 11, CROSS DEFENDANTS OLD REPUBLIC NATIONAL TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY, OLD REPUBLIC TITLE COMPANY'S DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT Old Republic Title Company's (Old Republic Title) demurrer to the 1st, 2nd, and 6th causes of action in D 27's first amended cross-complaint (FACC) is overruled in its entirety. Old Republic Title's request for judicial notice is denied. *THE COURTS FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO ALL COUNSEL* As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. rulings.Case Title: PALO ALTO NETWORKS, INC. VS. VIRGIN HOTELS SAN FRANCISCO, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 25, 2021, Line 9, DEFENDANT VIRGIN HOTELS SAN FRANCISCO, LLC'S MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE The verified application of Bryan T. Mohler for admission as counsel pro hac vice to defendant is granted. No opposition filed, and the application conforms to Cal. R. Ct. 9.40. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing. =(302/EPS)
